text:
IMPARTIAL NEWS + INTELLIGENT DEBATE

Account

We are officially into the final weekend of theParis Olympicsas the clock ticks down to Sunday’s closing ceremony.

Team GBhave already surpassed their minimum medal target of 55 going into the last throes of competition, but there could be plenty more on the way.

British athletes are hoping to be in contention in the 4x400m in both the men’s and women’s category after Noah Williams won bronze in the men’s 10m platform diving final.

Georgia Bell also beat Laura Muir to 1,500m bronze in the women’s final, running a national record time, before GB’s runners added a third and fourth bronze in the men’s and women’s 4x400m relay.

And in the pool, Kate Shortman and Isabelle Thorpe earned Team GB’s first ever artistic swimming medal, finishing with a shock silver.

This live blog is now closed.

Well that’s it from the final Saturday of Paris 2024, and what a day it’s been.

Six medals for Team GB – four bronzes and two silvers, including that historic first artistic silver prize.

We’ll be back for the final day of these Games tomorrow with all the action from the cycling, modern pentathlon and closing ceremony.

Silver for 21-year-old Caden Cunningham. Salimi showed his greater experience to ease to gold in the end.

Still, it’s the 63rd medal for Team GB – hitting the pre-Games target.

Salimi, the taller man, storms back to win the second round 9-1. Got to be favourite now. Cunningham needs some big energy in the third and final round.

First round to Cunningham.

Mind you, Rebecca McGowan won the opener in her bronze medal match and lost out in a tight third. Let’s see him shut it out in two for gold.

Caden Cunningham under way against Arian Salimi in the taekwondo 80kg final. Lands the first scoring point, concedes one to the head, and lands again to the body.

Quick start from both fighters.

Caden Cunningham is nearly 25 minutes late in starting his +80kg gold medal match, but the Terminator emerges at the Grand Palais.

He will face Iran’s Arian Salimi for Team GB’s first ever men’s taekwondo gold.

Rebecca McGowan is beaten at the death and Nafia Kus takes bronze. Disappointing for the Brit but she’ll be back.

One thing Paris have really messed up is the medal ceremonies at the athletics. Before or in this case after every session, there are four or five medal ceremonies one after another, which I think really lose some of their specialness when there rare, as there are tonight, eight in a row over the course of an hour with nothing else going on.

The stadium will be mostly empty by the time the 4x400m relay runners come through to pick up their bronzes.

Nafia Kus wins the second round 4-2. Rebecca McGowan will have to win a decider if she wants Olympic bronze.

Rebecca McGowan has won the first round of her +67kg bronze medal taekwondo match 9-7 – she is only one round from an Olympic medal.

What has the Dunbarton star got now?

Rebecca McGowan is up first of the two GB medal fights in the taekwondo, taking on Turkey’s Nafia Kus Aydin in the bronze medal clash.

She had to come through the repechage to get here after being initially knocked out in the quarter finals, but her +67kg fight is about to begin at the Grand Palais.

This reaction 🥹Kate Shortman and Isabelle Thorpe are guaranteed a medal in the  women's duet!The first-ever for@TeamGBin artistic swimming.#BBCOlympics#Paris2024#Olympicspic.twitter.com/aRkJj2cHGq

Another bronze for the Brits in the 4x400m, Amber Anning just overhauled in the final few metres by who else but Femke Bol.

Of course the Americans won it, and came within a tenth of a second of breaking a world record that has stood for 36 years.

The women’s 4x400m relay is under way.

Hard to understate how significant that is. Kate Shortman and Isabelle Thorpe have won silver for Team GB in the artistic swimming, the nation’s first ever.

What an achievement. They produced the highest score in the free routine section of the final. Stunning.

A high jump champion at last! And it’s Hamish Kerr of New Zealand who sprints into the middle of the field in celebration.

Good thing the javelin has finished…

Oh what a race! USA and Botswana ago toe-to-toe the whole way down and the Americans win it by a tenth of a second.

Charlie Dobson was never going to be able to run down the two ahead of him, they had 10 metres to start with, but he secures bronze for Great Britain and the team have broken the European record too, running 2:55.83.

Gasps in the stadium though as France were shoved off the track in the final lap, tangling with Belgium and South Africa.

Well they’ve both failed at 2.36m and the bar is going down to 2.34m for gold, which is the same height Sottile in fourth cleared, bizarrely.

All a bit weird but we have the 4x400m relay first anyway, so they will wait for that to finish.

Shortman and Thorpe perform their “Rising Phoenix” routine, apparently representing the rebirth of artistic swimming.

They look pretty synchronised to me. They’re guaranteed a medal – they get 294.5085 for their routine, the highest of the day so far.

Two pairings to go. Where will the British pair finish? They’re pioneers regardless.

Erm, there is some confusion in the high jump. The monitors had been showing Hamish Kerr as the leader on his own, on countback, and that has now been revised for him to be tied for first with Shelby McEwen.

They have both failed three times at 2.38m – but there is no shared gold this time: instead, it is a jump-off for gold. The bar stays at 2.38m and it is basically sudden death now.

Curious.

Impartial news + intelligent debate

title: Athletics finale triggers medal rush as Cunningham earns taekwondo silver
author: George Simms, Kat Lucas
description: Follow's i's live blog from day 15 of the Olympic Games in Paris
